properties.titulo_evento: Oil prices jump as US and Iran trade attacks over Strait of Hormuz
properties.descricao: Brent crude climbs more than 4 percent as Washington and Tehran clash over control of critical waterway.

properties.titulo_evento: US launches more strikes on Iran as Strait of Hormuz standoff deepens
properties.descricao: The US has launched a new wave of strikes on Iran targeting Tehran's ability to threaten shipping in the strait.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war live: Tehran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan after US bombings
properties.descricao: Iranian state media report explosions in port cities of Bandar Abbas, Sirik and Jask, as well as Qeshm Island.

properties.titulo_evento: US launches new wave of strikes against Iran, aimed at ‘degrading’ military
properties.descricao: Sunday's attacks follow three previous days of tit-for-tat strikes between Iran and US as a June ceasefire deal frays.

properties.titulo_evento: Detained by settlers, US Democrat Ro Khanna now faces pro-Israel attacks
properties.descricao: Progressive congressman accuses Israeli military of 'lying' after settlers held up his van in the occupied West Bank.

properties.titulo_evento: Israel releases video of huge detonations in Lebanon
properties.descricao: The Israeli military has released video showing huge explosions and detonations in villages in southern Lebanon.

properties.titulo_evento: Deadly Israeli strikes pound Gaza
properties.descricao: Israel’s military has attacked several places across Gaza.

properties.titulo_evento: Israel to hold national elections on October 27, parliament says
properties.descricao: Netanyahu, Israel's longest-serving prime minister, has confirmed that he will run for office again.

properties.titulo_evento: Lindsey Graham, hardline backer of US-Israeli wars, dies aged 71
properties.descricao: US senator Lindsey Graham, a key ally of Israel and one of Washington’s most prominent foreign policy hawks, has died.

properties.titulo_evento: Nine dead as Russia and Ukraine trade drone and missile salvos
properties.descricao: Ukraine is particularly vulnerable to Russian ballistic missiles due to critical shortage of Patriot munitions.

properties.titulo_evento: US and Iran trade fire as tensions rise over Strait of Hormuz
properties.descricao: Within hours of fresh US attacks, Iran said it had struck US military bases in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain.

properties.titulo_evento: T. rex could become most expensive fossil ever - but it's a ​problem for scientists
properties.descricao: A 67 million-year-old Tyrannosaurus rex will go on sale in New York with a pre-sale value of $30m.

properties.titulo_evento: Polish PM pledges memorial to victims of WW2 'genocide by Ukrainian nationalists'
properties.descricao: Warsaw and Kyiv have for decades been at loggerheads over the killings of many thousands of civilians during World War Two.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump administration subpoenas New York Times journalists over Air Force One reporting
properties.descricao: The reporters received the legal summons after they reported on alleged security issues with the president's new plane, which was gifted by Qatar.

properties.titulo_evento: The Chinese graduate accused of being Mexico's 'fentanyl king'
properties.descricao: Cartel members and ex-colleagues describe how Zhang Zhidong allegedly linked Chinese chemicals to Mexican drug labs.

properties.titulo_evento: US launches more strikes on Iran as renewed standoff over Strait of Hormuz intensifies
properties.descricao: Iranian state media reported US strikes targeting large areas across southern and western Iran, including Qeshm island and Bandar Abbas near the strait, and in Khuzestan province bordering Iraq. Missile alert sirens also sounded at dawn Monday in Bah...

properties.titulo_evento: Spain wildfire: Firefighters gain ground as Almería evacuees return home
properties.descricao: Firefighters gained the upper hand on the deadly Almería wildfire after days of battling the flames, as the blaze that killed 12 people and burned 66 square kilometres began to ease, allowing some of the nearly 1,500 evacuees to return home.

properties.titulo_evento: Israel to hold elections on 27 October in what is seen as referendum on Netanyahu leadership
properties.descricao: Israel will hold national elections on 27 October, the last date allowed by law to hold national elections. The Knesset is set to end its current term on 17 July.

properties.titulo_evento: France: hundreds dive into the Seine for Paris’ first river race in 80 years
properties.descricao: Swimmers plunged into the Seine near the Eiffel Tower for the Open Swim Harmonie Mutuelle, the first major organised race on the river in nearly 80 years.

properties.titulo_evento: Argentina fans erupt as Messi's men reach World Cup semifinal
properties.descricao: Argentina fans celebrate in Buenos Aires after a dramatic 3-1 extra-time win over ten-man Switzerland sends the defending champions into the World Cup semifinals.

properties.titulo_evento: US Senator Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
properties.descricao: Graham, a prominent Republican voice on foreign policy, had recently returned from Ukraine, where he met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy in Kyiv.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran attacks Gulf states to impose its Hormuz battle lines
properties.descricao: Iran launched waves of attacks across the Gulf in response to US airstrikes the night before, signalling the Islamic Republic’s refreshed strike capabilities, while claiming sole control of the Strait of Hormuz. The Gulf states are once again on the ...

properties.titulo_evento: Trump threatens to 'completely decimate' Iran, a day after claiming talks to take place
properties.descricao: "1,000 missiles are locked and loaded and aimed at the Islamic Republic of Iran," Trump wrote on social media.

properties.titulo_evento: War in Ukraine: Kyiv hit by Russian ballistic missiles and drone swarm, 10 injured
properties.descricao: Ukraine woke to fresh destruction on 11 July after Russia launched overnight strikes on Kyiv, leaving at least 10 people injured, including a child, according to Ukrainian officials.

properties.titulo_evento: Spain score late winner to beat Belgium and set up clash with France
properties.descricao: Spain will now go on to face Didier Deschamps' France - arguably the tournament's standout performer so far - in Dallas in the semifinals on Tuesday.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukrainian drone strikes kill at least 3, wound 5 in Moscow region
properties.descricao: Drone attacks killed at least three people and injured five others in the Moscow region, governor Andrey Vorobyov said on Telegram Monday. Ukraine has been intensifying its strikes on Russia's energy targets in response to Moscow's barrages.

properties.titulo_evento: Israel sets October 27 election as Netanyahu faces crucial post-Gaza war test
properties.descricao: Israel will hold national elections on Oct. 27, the latest date permitted by law, parliament said on Sunday, setting up a vote widely seen as a referendum on Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's leadership since the Gaza war began. Netanyahu, 76, sai...

properties.titulo_evento: Middle East live: US strikes Iran as Gulf states targeted in flareup over Hormuz
properties.descricao: The US military on Sunday said it had launched a new wave of attacks against Iran, after renewed fighting over the Strait of Hormuz saw several of Washington's Gulf allies targeted by incoming fire. The flare-up is the latest to undermine an interim...

properties.titulo_evento: Iran expands attacks on Gulf states after intense US strikes
properties.descricao: Iran reported strikes Sunday evening on two of its southern islands while Kuwait, where Tehran has repeatedly targeted US installations, said border posts and an offshore oil platform had been attacked. The as-yet unclaimed strikes came hours after ...

properties.titulo_evento: Jannik Sinner beats Alexander Zverev to win second straight Wimbledon title
properties.descricao: Italy's Jannik Sinner won his second consecutive title after his German opponent appeared bothered by a knee issue following a slip to the grass at a key point in the third set. Sinner's fifth Grand Slam title came in his first tournament since a se...

properties.titulo_evento: Hundreds evacuated from Paris suburb after weapons found near synagogue
properties.descricao: Three hundred people were evacuated on Saturday evening from a neighbourhood in Sarcelles, north of Paris, following reports of a suspicious vehicle, which was found to contain guns, including "a military-grade weapon", according interior minister L...

properties.titulo_evento: Macron calls for 'constant vigilance' against antisemitism as France honors Alfred Dreyfus

properties.titulo_evento: Are tanks ageing like the cavalry in WW1?
properties.descricao: The rapid pace of technological advances in drone warfare, guided by artificial intelligence in Ukraine and elsewhere in the world, has fundamentally changed the face of conflict. There is a question about how radical that change is: an evolution or...

properties.titulo_evento: Lindsey Graham: 'He was the one who justified Israel's actions'
properties.descricao: Graham often advised Trump on foreign affairs, particularly on matters pertaining to Israel, Ukraine and Iran. He was a frequent visitor at the White House. Shirli Sitbon tells us more.

properties.titulo_evento: Mathieu Van der Poel overcomes heat to win Tour de France stage nine
properties.descricao: 31-year-old Dutchman and cobbled classics specialist Mathieu van der Poel claimed his third Tour de France win on Sunday with victory in the ninth stage, which was shortened due to intense heat. The former world champion, won a sprint amongst his th...

properties.titulo_evento: France temporarily shuts down three nuclear reactors over heatwave
properties.descricao: France's main energy provider on Sunday said that three nuclear reactors have been temporarily shut down, while eight others are operating at reduced power. The measure is an environmental protection requirement to avoid discharging too much hot wat...

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war: US carries out fresh strikes near Strait of Hormuz
properties.descricao: The US army said the strikes near the vital waterway were intended to "degrade" Tehran's ability to attack commercial ships. Iran has retaliated by targeting US allies in the region. Follow DW for the latest.

properties.titulo_evento: Venezuela sets up temporary housing, as death toll rises to almost 4,500
properties.descricao: As the official death toll from the devastating earthquakes which struck northern Venezuela last month continues to rise, authorities are planning to set up temporary housing to shelter thousands of people left homeless.

properties.titulo_evento: France opens terror probe after gun found in Jewish suburb
properties.descricao: Police said they found a "military-grade weapon" during the search of a suspicious car parked in a Paris neighborhood with a large Jewish population.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany's Ruhr Valley is on brink of financial collapse
properties.descricao: With tax revenues down and social spending up, more and more cities and towns in the Ruhrgebiet are struggling to make ends meet. One city that is particularly deep in the red is Oberhausen.

properties.titulo_evento: US Senator Lindsey Graham dies
properties.descricao: The South Carolina politician and staunch ally of Donald Trump died aged 71 after ​a brief and sudden illness, his office said. The hawkish senator backed the 2003 invasion of Iraq and the more recent war with Iran.

properties.titulo_evento: Gas queues grow as Ukraine targets Russia's fuel supply
properties.descricao: Attacks by Ukraine have triggered a fuel crisis in Russia. Reports from across Russia show how residents are dealing with the disruptions in their daily lives.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war: US claims Strait of Hormuz is 'open' amid strikes
properties.descricao: US President Trump and the US military's Central Command insisted on Sunday that the Strait of Hormuz is open despite the Iranian navy declaring it closed. Both sides continue to exchange strikes.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany's richest man takes on Big Tech
properties.descricao: Dieter Schwarz made his fortune from supermarkets, becoming Germany's wealthiest person. Now he wants to take on Google, Microsoft and Amazon and an entire region stands to benefit.

properties.titulo_evento: Why Europe still can’t agree on Israel
properties.descricao: EU foreign ministers meet in Brussels as a growing group of countries push the European Commission to propose restrictions on trade with illegal Israeli settlements. Zoya Sheftalovich and Nick Vinocur explain why although the economic impact would be...

properties.titulo_evento: Israel-critical capitals force von der Leyen showdown over illegal settlements
properties.descricao: A push for tougher limits on trade with West Bank settlements is set to dominate a gathering of EU foreign ministers in Brussels.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine sees opportune moment to pressure Putin before winter
properties.descricao: Kyiv and its allies believe deep strikes on Russian fuel infrastructure have given Ukraine a battlefield edge — which they now need to turn into leverage.

properties.titulo_evento: Captured African recruits say they want to go home — to Russia
properties.descricao: African POWs have become pawns in Moscow and Kyiv’s battle for influence.

properties.titulo_evento: European leaders mourn US Senator Lindsey Graham
properties.descricao: The South Carolina Republican strongly advocated for sanctions against Russia over its war on Ukraine.

properties.titulo_evento: Oil Prices Surge After Iran and U.S. Trade Strikes
properties.descricao: The renewed hostilities posed fresh risks to ships seeking to navigate the Strait of Hormuz.

properties.titulo_evento: How Putin Turned Japan Into a Den of Spies
properties.descricao: Operating out of a Tokyo high-rise, a military intelligence unit finds the high-tech equipment that Russia needs to wage war.

properties.titulo_evento: Inside the Secret Factory That Supplies Ukraine’s War Drones
properties.descricao: With its mass-produced and inexpensive A.I. powered war machines, the German start-up Helsing SE illustrates a profound shift in military spending.

properties.titulo_evento: How Volkswagen’s Troubles Were Made in China
properties.descricao: The German auto giant’s push into China powered it for decades, but now the company faces fierce competition from Chinese automakers in markets around the world.
